mission.title: Install and Configure Score on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Score Specification provides a developer-centric and platform-agnostic workload specification to improve developer productivity and experience. Installing Score allows you to define and manage your workloads in a consistent manner across different environments.

steps.title: Install the Score CLI
steps.description:
Download and install the Score CLI tool for Kubernetes. Score provides `score-k8s` and `score-compose` commands for translating workload specifications.
```bash
# Download the binary from GitHub releases
curl -sL https://github.com/score-spec/score-k8s/releases/latest/download/score-k8s_$(uname -s)_$(uname -m).tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv score-k8s /usr/local/bin/
score-k8s version
```

steps.title: Initialize a Score project
steps.description:
Initialize a new Score project in your working directory:
```bash
score-k8s init
```
This creates a `score.yaml` template that describes your workload in a platform-agnostic way.

steps.title: Create a Score workload specification
steps.description:
Create or edit `score.yaml` to define your workload:
```yaml
apiVersion: score.dev/v1b1
metadata:
  name: my-workload
containers:
  main:
    image: nginx:1.25
    ports:
      http:
        port: 80
        protocol: TCP
```

steps.title: Generate and deploy Kubernetes manifests
steps.description:
Use score-k8s to generate Kubernetes manifests and deploy:
```bash
score-k8s generate score.yaml | kubectl apply -f -
```

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
Check that your workload was deployed:
```bash
kubectl get pods
kubectl get deployments
kubectl describe deployment my-workload
```

resolution.summary: Score CLI is installed and you can translate Score workload specifications into Kubernetes manifests for deployment.

uninstall.title: Remove deployed workloads
uninstall.description:
Delete the resources created from Score:
```bash
score-k8s generate score.yaml | kubectl delete -f -
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Score CLI
uninstall.description:
Remove the Score CLI:
```bash
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/score-k8s
```

upgrade.title: Update Score CLI
upgrade.description:
Download the latest version:
```bash
curl -sL https://github.com/score-spec/score-k8s/releases/latest/download/score-k8s_$(uname -s)_$(uname -m).tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv score-k8s /usr/local/bin/
score-k8s version
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If your Score pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, you can check the logs to diagnose the issue. Use the following command to view the logs of a specific pod:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace score
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Score service, ensure that the service is running and check the port-forwarding command. Verify the service status with:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace score
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during the Helm upgrade, ensure that you are using a compatible version of the Score chart. You can check the available versions with:
```bash
helm search repo score --versions
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources for Score pods
troubleshooting.description:
If the Score pods are failing to start due to insufficient resources, you may need to adjust the resource limits in the deployment. Check the events for the pods to see resource-related errors:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace score
```

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubectl configured.
